isolation.only_effective_information_change: Each retained history event now includes its original issued query.
isolation.capacity_note: The enriched run uses max length 768 instead of 512 solely because a label-free tokenizer audit required it to preserve all six query-plus-item history events; candidate-only truncation remains allowed.

matched_model.recipe: The item-only KuaiSearch Full recipe was copied before evaluation; no development label selected a hyperparameter.

comparison_with_item_only_history.conclusion: Restoring the missing behavioral query changes neither the practical true-over-null utility nor the very low conversion of fixed response budget into correct nonrepeat ranking direction.

interpretation.supported: On natural-query strict-nonrepeat requests, an ordinary full-token Transformer moves almost every candidate pair after reading a complete query-item history, yet the movement is chance-directional and converts only about four percent of the available label-aligned response headroom.
interpretation.falsified_alternative: The KuaiSearch gap is mainly an adapter artifact caused by dropping the query attached to each historical action.
interpretation.positive_control: The same model and information object produce significantly correct direction and true-history utility when a candidate item recurs in history, so inactivity, serialization breakage and generic inability to read history are not plausible explanations.
interpretation.boundary: The independently trained FULL model still beats QC on strict-nonrepeat as a whole. The localized failure is therefore not 'history always hurts'; it is inefficient and unstable attribution of history-induced score motion to the right candidates.

next_action: Use JDsearch v2 as the independent functional PPS replication. Do not formulate an architecture until that outcome establishes whether the failure is cross-source or KuaiSearch-specific.
